description: Breaking-change bundle to decide together at 2.0 (from the 2026-07-02 review): (1) 'flow' (DSL context manager) vs 'Flow' (class) — README itself uses both names; importing both shadows one; consider renaming the CM (e.g. open_flow). (2) do(fn, *args, name=None, after=(), cache=True, **kwargs) reserves name/after/cache — a user fn taking those kwargs can't receive them; consider _name/_after or positional-only. (3) Naming asymmetry: engine best_of/with_retry fan over a collection via map; DSL fanout/retry wrap a single unit — 'fanout' sounds like map but isn't. (4) serve.parse_tunnel_url is pure back-compat with no in-repo caller — remove at 2.0.
title: 2.0 API seams: flow/Flow shadowing, do() kwarg collisions, fanout-vs-map naming
